DriverProfile.title: DriverProfile
DriverProfile.description: Represents a delivery driver's personal and operational information within the RutaRápida Driver application. This includes their identity, contact details, operational status, and a reference to their assigned vehicle.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DriverProfile entity.

externalUserId.description: Reference to the unique identifier from an external authentication system for the driver.

addressLine1.description: The first line of the driver's street address.

addressLine2.description: The second line of the driver's street address (e.g., apartment, suite number).

city.description: The city of the driver's address.

stateProvince.description: The state or province of the driver's address.

postalCode.description: The postal code of the driver's address.

country.description: The country of the driver's address.

profilePictureUrl.description: URL to the driver's profile picture.

vehicleId.description: Reference to the Vehicle entity currently assigned to this driver. (Relationship: DriverProfile 1:1 Vehicle)

availabilityStatus.description: The current operational status of the driver (e.g., 'Available', 'Offline', 'OnDelivery', 'Break').

rating.description: The average rating of the driver based on customer feedback.

totalDeliveries.description: The total number of deliveries completed by the driver.

registrationDate.description: The date and time when the driver registered with the application.

lastOnline.description: The last known date and time when the driver was online or active in the application.

Vehicle.title: Vehicle
Vehicle.description: Details about a vehicle used by a driver for deliveries, including make, model, and registration information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Vehicle entity.

driverId.description: Reference to the DriverProfile entity that this vehicle is primarily assigned to. (Relationship: Vehicle 1:1 DriverProfile)

year.description: The manufacturing year of the vehicle.

licensePlate.description: The license plate number of the vehicle.

capacityDescription.description: A description of the vehicle's capacity (e.g., 'Small', 'Medium', 'Large' or specific dimensions).

insurancePolicyNumber.description: The insurance policy number for the vehicle.

registrationExpiryDate.description: The expiration date of the vehicle's registration.

DeliveryOrder.title: DeliveryOrder
DeliveryOrder.description: Represents a single delivery order, encompassing details about the assigned driver, locations, package, and current status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DeliveryOrder entity.

driverId.description: Reference to the DriverProfile entity assigned to this order. (Relationship: DriverProfile 1:N DeliveryOrder)

customerId.description: Reference to the unique identifier of the customer who placed the order (from an external system).

orderNumber.description: A human-readable order number for this delivery.

pickupLocationId.description: Reference to the Location entity where the package needs to be picked up. (Relationship: DeliveryOrder N:1 Location)

deliveryLocationId.description: Reference to the Location entity where the package needs to be delivered. (Relationship: DeliveryOrder N:1 Location)

packageId.description: Reference to the Package entity associated with this delivery order. (Relationship: DeliveryOrder 1:1 Package)

status.description: The current status of the delivery order (e.g., 'Pending', 'Assigned', 'Picked Up', 'In Transit', 'Delivered', 'Problem', 'Cancelled').

pickupInstructions.description: Special instructions for picking up the package.

deliveryInstructions.description: Special instructions for delivering the package.

estimatedPickupTime.description: The estimated date and time for package pickup.

actualPickupTime.description: The actual date and time when the package was picked up.

estimatedDeliveryTime.description: The estimated date and time for package delivery.

actualDeliveryTime.description: The actual date and time when the package was delivered.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the delivery order was created.

updatedAt.description: The last date and time when the delivery order was updated.

proofOfDeliveryUrl.description: URL to a photo or signature serving as proof of delivery.

problemDescription.description: A description of any problems encountered during delivery if the status is 'Problem'.

Location.title: Location
Location.description: Defines a geographical location with address details and coordinates, used for pickup and delivery points.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Location entity.

addressLine1.description: The first line of the street address.

addressLine2.description: The second line of the street address (e.g., apartment, suite number).

stateProvince.description: The state or province of the address.

postalCode.description: The postal code of the address.

latitude.description: The geographical latitude of the location.

longitude.description: The geographical longitude of the location.

contactName.description: The name of the contact person at this location.

contactPhoneNumber.description: The phone number of the contact person at this location.

Package.title: Package
Package.description: Contains detailed information about a package being delivered, such as its contents, dimensions, and tracking number.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Package entity.

deliveryOrderId.description: Reference to the DeliveryOrder entity this package belongs to. (Relationship: Package 1:1 DeliveryOrder)

description.description: A description of the package contents.

weight.description: The weight of the package in a standard unit (e.g., kg, lbs).

dimensions.description: The dimensions of the package (e.g., '10x15x5 cm').

fragile.description: Indicates if the package contains fragile items.

trackingNumber.description: The tracking number for the package.

declaredValue.description: The declared monetary value of the package contents.

Notification.title: Notification
Notification.description: Stores information about in-app alerts and notifications sent to drivers.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Notification entity.

driverId.description: Reference to the DriverProfile entity who is the recipient of this notification. (Relationship: DriverProfile 1:N Notification)

type.description: The type of notification (e.g., 'New Order', 'Order Update', 'Cancellation', 'Message', 'System Alert').

title.description: The title or subject of the notification.

body.description: The main content or message of the notification.

isRead.description: A boolean indicating whether the driver has read the notification.

sentAt.description: The date and time when the notification was sent.

deliveryOrderId.description: Optional reference to a DeliveryOrder if the notification is related to a specific order. (Relationship: Notification N:1 DeliveryOrder)

Message.title: Message
Message.description: Facilitates communication between drivers, customers, and dispatch, storing chat messages or call logs.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Message entity.

senderId.description: The ID of the sender. This could be a DriverProfile ID or an external ID for Customer/Dispatcher.

receiverId.description: The ID of the receiver. This could be a DriverProfile ID or an external ID for Customer/Dispatcher.

deliveryOrderId.description: Optional reference to a DeliveryOrder if the message is related to a specific order. (Relationship: Message N:1 DeliveryOrder)

content.description: The text content of the message.

sentAt.description: The date and time when the message was sent.

isReadByReceiver.description: A boolean indicating whether the receiver has read the message.

RouteOptimizationLog.title: RouteOptimizationLog
RouteOptimizationLog.description: Records the results and details of AI-powered route optimization sessions for drivers.

id.description: Unique identifier for the RouteOptimizationLog entity.

driverId.description: Reference to the DriverProfile entity for whom the route was optimized. (Relationship: DriverProfile 1:N RouteOptimizationLog)

deliveryOrderIds.description: An array of references to DeliveryOrder entities included in this optimized route. (Relationship: RouteOptimizationLog N:N DeliveryOrder)

originalRouteData.description: A serialized representation of the route before optimization (e.g., JSON string of waypoints).

optimizedRouteData.description: A serialized representation of the optimized route (e.g., JSON string of waypoints).

optimizationTimestamp.description: The date and time when the route optimization was performed.

efficiencyImprovementPercentage.description: The percentage improvement in efficiency (e.g., reduced distance or time) achieved by the optimization.

estimatedTimeSavedMinutes.description: The estimated number of minutes saved by using the optimized route.

algorithmUsed.description: The name or version of the AI algorithm used for optimization.

definition.description: Stores individual driver profiles. Each document ID '{driverId}' corresponds directly to the Firebase Authentication UID of the driver. This structure ensures explicit path-based ownership for a driver's private data, making authorization rules simple and highly debuggable. The 'externalUserId' field within the document will explicitly match the document ID and 'request.auth.uid', reinforcing authorization independence.

params.name: driverId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the driver.

definition.description: Stores vehicle details for a specific driver. Each vehicle document is nested under the owning driver's profile, providing clear, path-based ownership. The 'driverId' field within the document is denormalized to match the parent path parameter '{driverId}' and 'request.auth.uid', ensuring authorization independence and enabling efficient, secure list queries (QAPs) for a driver's vehicles.

params.name: driverId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the driver.

params.name: vehicleId
params.description: The unique identifier for the vehicle.

definition.description: Manages individual delivery orders assigned to a specific driver. This hierarchical structure ensures that all order-related operations are scoped to the authenticated driver. The 'driverId' field within the document is denormalized to match the '{driverId}' in the path and 'request.auth.uid', achieving Authorization Independence and facilitating secure listing of orders (QAPs) for the driver.

params.name: driverId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the driver.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier for the delivery order.

definition.description: Stores the pickup location details for a specific delivery order. Nested under the order and driver, this design denormalizes location data for each driver's order. The document will include denormalized 'driverId' (matching the path's '{driverId}') and 'deliveryOrderId' (matching the path's '{orderId}') to ensure Authorization Independence, allowing rules to verify access directly from the document data without cross-collection 'get()' calls. This approach supports QAPs by making access implicitly scoped.

params.name: driverId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the driver.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier for the delivery order.

definition.description: Stores the delivery location details for a specific delivery order. Nested under the order and driver, this design denormalizes location data for each driver's order. The document will include denormalized 'driverId' (matching the path's '{driverId}') and 'deliveryOrderId' (matching the path's '{orderId}') to ensure Authorization Independence, allowing rules to verify access directly from the document data without cross-collection 'get()' calls. This approach supports QAPs by making access implicitly scoped.

params.name: driverId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the driver.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier for the delivery order.

definition.description: Contains package details for a specific delivery order. Nested within the order and driver, this structure denormalizes package information. The document will include denormalized 'driverId' (matching the path's '{driverId}') and 'deliveryOrderId' (matching the path's '{orderId}') to ensure Authorization Independence and efficient QAPs, without needing 'get()' operations to check parent permissions.

params.name: driverId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the driver.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier for the delivery order.

definition.description: Manages in-app alerts and notifications specific to a driver. Nested under the driver's profile, this ensures that each driver can only access their own notifications. The 'driverId' field within the document is denormalized, matching the path's '{driverId}' and 'request.auth.uid', providing Authorization Independence and secure QAPs for notification lists.

params.name: driverId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the driver.

params.name: notificationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the notification.

definition.description: Facilitates direct communication (chat/call logs) for a specific driver. Nested under the driver's profile, messages can be order-related (with 'deliveryOrderId' field) or general. The document will include denormalized 'driverId' (matching path and 'request.auth.uid'), 'senderId', and 'receiverId' to ensure Authorization Independence and support QAPs, allowing a driver to access only messages where they are the sender or receiver.

params.name: driverId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the driver.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique identifier for the message.

definition.description: Records AI-powered route optimization results specific to a driver. Nested under the driver's profile, this structure ensures that each driver can only access their own optimization logs. The 'driverId' field within the document is denormalized to match the path's '{driverId}' and 'request.auth.uid', providing Authorization Independence and secure QAPs for log lists.

params.name: driverId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the driver.

params.name: logId
params.description: The unique identifier for the route optimization log.

definition.description: A top-level collection used for Database Access Control (DBAC) to define system administrators. The existence of a document at this path signifies that the '{adminId}' (Firebase Auth UID) holds an administrator role. Access is determined by document existence, avoiding 'get()' calls for authorization checks (except for the initial role check). Note: 'DriverProfile' schema is used as a placeholder for a simple document indicating existence; the actual document content can be minimal.

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of an administrator.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for RutaRápida Driver prioritizes **Authorization Independence** and **Queryable Authorization Patterns (QAPs)** by adopting a deeply hierarchical, driver-centric model. All driver-specific data is nested under `/driverProfiles/{driverId}`, where `{driverId}` directly corresponds to the authenticated user's Firebase UID (`request.auth.uid`).

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization (Mandate A):**
To eliminate the need for costly and complex `get()` calls in security rules, every document that is part of a subcollection of `/driverProfiles/{driverId}` will contain a denormalized `driverId` field in its data. This `driverId` field will explicitly match the `{driverId}` in its parent path, as well as `request.auth.uid`. For example, `DeliveryOrder` documents under `/driverProfiles/{driverId}/deliveryOrders/{orderId}` will have `resource.data.driverId == driverId`. This strategy is applied across `Vehicle`, `DeliveryOrder`, `Notification`, `Message`, and `RouteOptimizationLog` entities. This ensures that security rules can always determine authorization directly from the `request.auth.uid` and the document itself, rather than needing to inspect parent documents.

Critically, for entities like `Location` and `Package`, which are referenced by `DeliveryOrder` but don't inherently contain a `driverId` in their base schema, a key denormalization strategy is applied. Instead of keeping them as global collections that would require `get()` calls to check associated `DeliveryOrder` permissions, they are structured as subcollections of `DeliveryOrder` (e.g., `/driverProfiles/{driverId}/deliveryOrders/{orderId}/pickupLocation`). Within these nested `Location` and `Package` documents, the `driverId` and `deliveryOrderId` are explicitly denormalized. This ensures that a driver's access to a specific `pickupLocation`, `deliveryLocation`, or `package` is always verified directly from the document's own data and path, adhering strictly to the Authorization Independence principle.

**Queryable Authorization Patterns (QAPs) (Principle 4):**
The hierarchical structure directly supports efficient and secure `list` operations (QAPs).
*   **Driver-Specific Data**: By nesting all personal data and assigned work under `/driverProfiles/{driverId}`, a driver can query their own `vehicles`, `deliveryOrders`, `notifications`, `messages`, and `routeOptimizationLogs` by simply referencing their `uid` in the path. Security rules can then easily enforce `allow list: if request.auth.uid == driverId;`, ensuring that `list` operations automatically filter to only show data owned by the authenticated driver without exposing other drivers' information.
*   **Denormalization for Filtering**: The denormalized `driverId` field within documents allows for server-side `where('driverId', '==', request.auth.uid)` clauses, which are fully supported by security rules, guaranteeing that complex queries within a driver's scope remain secure and efficient.

**DBAC (Database Access Control) (Principle 3):**
Global roles, such as 'admin', are managed using dedicated top-level collections like `/roles_admin/{adminId}`. The mere existence of a document at `/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid)` grants the user administrative privileges. This avoids custom claims and utilizes Firestore's inherent existence checks, satisfying the DBAC principle and providing a clear, debuggable mechanism for role-based access.

**Structural Segregation (Mandate B):**
Each collection path is designed to hold documents with a homogeneous security posture. For example, a driver's `notifications` are distinct from their `deliveryOrders`, but both are private to the driver. This simplifies security rules by dedicating specific access patterns to specific data locations.

**Data Clarity and Predictability (Mandate D):**
*   **Explicit State**: `DeliveryOrder` uses a dedicated `status` field for clear state management.
*   **Predictable Schema**: Dynamic keys are avoided.
*   **Radical Consistency**: Wildcards are descriptive (e.g., `{driverId}`, `{orderId}`), and authorization fields like `driverId` are consistently used for ownership.
